name: Ibi and Sierra de Onil Loop
name_original: Sierra de Onil — Circular desde Ibi por trialeras
description: Challenging 27.3-mile mountain bike loop from Ibi through Sierra de Onil, featuring technical trialeras and scenic pine forests.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ibi offers diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains and deep gorges to extensive pine forests. The region is characterized by varied terrain, making it ideal for outdoor enthusiasts seeking adventure. Proximity to natural parks like Sierra de Mariola and El Carrascal de la Font Roja provides a network of trails through verdant environments and impressive rock formations. This area features a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents across its varied topography. Best mountain bike trails around Ibi The most popular mountain bike trail is Ibi and Sierra de Onil Loop, a 27.3 miles (43.9 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route explores the varied terrain surrounding Ibi and the Sierra de Onil. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Carrasqueta Pass – La Sarga Rock Paintings loop from Ibi, a moderate 23.0 miles (37.1 km) path. This trail features mountain passes offering views and passes by historical rock paintings. Local mountain bikers also love the Esbarzerets Refuge – Alt de la Martina loop from Ibi, a 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Mountain biking around Ibi is defined by challenging mountain terrain, serene natural parks, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Ibi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ibi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Ibi?
A: Ibi offers a comprehensive network of over 60 mountain bike trails. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains to serene natural parks, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Ibi?
A: Yes, Ibi has trails suitable for beginners. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are 4 easy trails available. These typically feature less technical terrain and more manageable ascents, allowing new riders to enjoy the scenic surroundings.

Q: What options are there for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find plenty of challenging options in Ibi, with 35 trails rated as difficult. These routes often include significant elevation gains and technical sections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927432404/ibi-and-sierra-de-onil-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibi and Sierra de Onil Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 43.9 km route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there many circular mountain bike routes around Ibi?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Ibi are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3968286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esbarzerets Refuge – Alt de la Martina loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 30.4 km moderate trail through mountainous landscapes.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Ibi?
A: Mountain biking in Ibi often takes you through or near stunning natural features. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cint&lt;/a&gt; gorge, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1023814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Font Roja&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3116128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mariola Spring&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3994612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Menejador&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2561516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó and Sit Mountains&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that combine mountain biking with cultural or historical sights?
A: Yes, some routes offer a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21955346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass – La Sarga Rock Paintings loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, featuring mountain passes with expansive views and passing by ancient rock paintings.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Ibi?
A: Ibi's diverse landscapes make it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters are mild but can bring cooler temperatures to higher mountain passes.

Q: Are mountain bike trails in Ibi generally dog-friendly?
A: While many natural areas in Spain welcome dogs, specific rules can vary, especially within natural parks like Sierra de Mariola and El Carrascal de la Font Roja. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash and check local regulations for the specific trail or park you plan to visit to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ibi?
A: Mountain bike trails in Ibi often feature significant elevation changes due to the region's mountainous terrain. For instance, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33939837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Antoni Tunnel – L'Estepar Tunnel loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt; can involve over 850 meters of ascent, providing a good workout and rewarding views.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Ibi suitable for e-bikes?
A: Yes, Ibi is considered e-bike friendly. The region offers 21 trails that are suitable for e-bikes, allowing riders to tackle the varied terrain and enjoy the scenic climbs with assistance.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ibi?
A: The mountain bike trails in Ibi are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders frequently praise the challenging mountain terrain, the beauty of the natural parks, and the extensive pine forests that define the area's routes.

Q: Do any mountain bike trails pass through natural parks?
A: Absolutely. Many mountain bike trails in Ibi are located within or adjacent to significant natural parks, such as the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park and El Carrascal de la Font Roja Natural Park. These parks offer extensive trail networks through diverse landscapes, including pine forests, gorges, and areas with abundant springs.
tips: I would try not to go on the weekend. The site is very beautiful but there are usually a lot of people and some steps are narrow.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Sierra de Onil — Circular desde Ibi por trialeras mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is characterized by its 'trialeras,' meaning you'll encounter challenging technical singletrack sections. The paths often consist of dirt and loose stones, with some parts featuring steep ascents, averaging up to 27% gradient. While there might be some road or asphalt sections, a significant portion is on varied terrain, including both narrow singletrack and wider tracks.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is explicitly rated as a difficult mountain bike ride. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for sections where they may need to dismount and push their bikes due to the challenging and complex terrain, with technical difficulty rated between STS S3 and S6.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this challenging mountain bike route is approximately 4 hours and 30 minutes. However, actual times can vary significantly based on your fitness level, riding speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or navigate technical sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de Onil — Circular desde Ibi por trialeras trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Ibi. You'll need to find suitable parking within Ibi to begin your ride. Specific parking areas for mountain bikers are usually available near the town's outskirts or designated public parking zones, but it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What are the notable natural features or scenery along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect great landscapes and beautiful pine woods full of granite rocks. The Sierra de Onil is part of the picturesque mountains surrounding the Hoya de Castalla. The route offers pleasant views of pine forests, cultivated fields, and traditional houses, especially between Ibi and Onil. You'll also pass by highlights like the Barranco de los Molinos and the Pou del Barber (Cava Blanca).

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride along parts of the Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós, Vuelta al la hoya por el Menejador, Camí dels Geladors, and the Cami del Xocolatero Trail. Other smaller segments include El surcol, de cava del barber al raco vell, and the GR-7.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Onil — Circular desde Ibi por trialeras trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the region, it's crucial to remember that this is a difficult mountain bike route with technical sections. If you bring your dog, it must be fit enough to keep up, accustomed to challenging terrain, and kept on a leash, especially within the Parc Natural del Carrascar de la Font Roja, to protect wildlife and ensure safety for all trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The region of Alicante generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. However, to avoid extreme summer heat and potential winter cold or rain, the best times are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these periods, temperatures are milder, making the challenging ascents more manageable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail, especially within Parc Natural del Carrascar de la Font Roja?
faqs.answer: This trail passes through the Parc Natural del Carrascar de la Font Roja. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails within the natural park. However, it's always advisable to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be aware of any temporary restrictions or conservation efforts in place. No specific permits for cycling are mentioned for this area.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or specific highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Beyond the general beautiful landscapes, you'll encounter several points of interest. Keep an eye out for the Barranco de los Molinos, the Pou del Barber (Cava Blanca), and the unique Green Tunnel. The trail also includes sections of the Cami del Xocolatero Trail and passes through an area known as Cuatro Caminos.
